content:
Judgment names
the hand.
It does not mend
the thing.
A verdict is a nail
held over the board.
It is not the hammer.
The deed stands
where it was placed.
The room knows.
The body knows.
The broken door
does not close
because the mouth
has learned confession.
First:
stop the hand.
Then:
stop asking
the struck thing
to explain the bruise.
Name the deed
without weather.
No storm did this.
No wound did this.
The wound may have shaken
the hand.
The hand still arrived.
This is the line.
After the line
comes labor.
Pick up the glass.
Pay for the door.
Return the money.
Make the call
the injured person
should not have had to make.
Carry the weight
back toward
its source.
That is restitution.
Not sorrow.
Not performance.
Not apology
as a second demand.
Not a confession
asking to be carried.
Forgiveness belongs
to the one
who was asked
to survive you.
Trust does not return
because it is summoned.
Trust returns,
if it returns,
to a place
that has become safer.
Some things
do not come back.
Time does not.
The first body
before fear
does not.
The old room
does not.
Do not demand
that the mended thing
pretend
it was never broken.
A seam is not failure.
A seam is memory
doing its work.
Let it show
where care must go.
Do not call distance
cruelty
when distance
is what safety costs.
Build the guardrail
where the car
left the road.
Move the knife.
Give up the key.
Leave before
the old command
finds your mouth.
Call the number
while the hand
is still only shaking.
The past is not yours
to revise.
The consequence
is not yours
to resent.
The forgiveness
is not yours
to collect.
The next act is.
The next distance.
The next warning.
The next refusal.
The next repair
made before
an audience appears.
The one who broke it
does not owe
a perfect life.
They owe
a different pattern.
They owe the seam
conditions
under which it does not
have to split again.

AN INVENTORY OF WHAT YOU DON’T OWN — the series
The Machine That Knows It Ends
condition inventory: the inherited limits of perception, appetite, fear, language, tribe, and mortality
What Is Ours
custody position: what remains in custody after the world has entered through damaged lenses
The Surveyor
boundary position: the exact field of responsibility, neither borrowed guilt nor abandoned ground
The Remaining Lever
intervention position: the narrow available refusal between inherited pressure and enacted harm
The Seam Going Forward
restitution position: the conditions owed to the damaged thing after judgment has named the hand
. . .
What arrives.
What remains.
What is bound.
What can refuse.
What repair owes.
